title: podman play kube --replace does not work when piped
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When piping a kube yaml file into play kube it does not work with `--replace` as it seems /dev/stdin is already read before and therefore empty.



**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
[jelle@t14s][~/projects/console.dot]%cat kube.yml | podman play kube --network consoledot --replace  -                                                                                 [54/1996]
Error: /dev/stdin: YAML document does not contain any supported kube kind
[jelle@t14s][~/projects/console.dot]%cat kube.yml | podman play kube --network consoledot  -
Pod:
737776d9502295ffbb02deebb926f7514c9967c6512f3ebc4d0533eaf50edb6f
Containers:
318cf4de3fe41e2f59d0c5aab119571af1c13dcf6439516af2a6512ca4e64703
18293db147cb992b835cc129ba1f75c8d6c01a0e8eed0715ce27a1e265da846c

Pod:
e7df2a753adcf20f08f791713da920dd4cc5ea87559cb9f0fc42dcea77839443
Container:
4bf7f0d236755152727d167cffb9fbe7fd642370e39cdab89bb299a80fb81728
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Pods are not created

**Describe the results you expected:**

Pods are created

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.2.0
```

comments.body: This actually works already in `main` as it was fixed in 1a69e285813ea6d26c7da06d4e12230826ddb19e
